name: Drift Baseline, Monitoring, Incident Runbook, And Owners

releaseAnnotation.scope: Post E-1/E-2/E-3 release verification baseline and monitoring handoff for https://www.mitra-auto.fi
affectedSurfaces: legacy redirects and product identifier redirects
private, checkout, account, and error routes
rendered booking, contact, catalog, product, cart, checkout, and accessibility journeys from E-3

currentBaselineDecision: E-4 can close as an operating baseline and handoff, not as launch approval. The monitored baseline records known production failures so remediation can be verified and future drift can be detected.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Production static SEO assets are not served correctly: /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml return 404; product sitemap and Merchant feed URLs return HTML.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Live HTTP matrix shows correct 200 XML/text assets with expected content type and body, and Search Console/Merchant Center readback confirms fetchability when platform access is available.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Production public routes serve generic raw SPA shell without route-specific canonical/schema; rendered pages hydrate later but raw HTTP remains weak for release policy.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Raw curl and rendered browser parity both show route-specific title, description, canonical, robots, hreflang where applicable, H1, crawlable links, and JSON-LD.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Private/admin/account routes still return public HTTP 200 shell at the edge.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Unauthenticated /cms, account, admin, and customer routes return safe 401/403/404 or protected noindex policy with no private content.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Legacy service/catalog routes and opaque product identifier URLs do not perform permanent one-hop redirects in production.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Redirect sample returns 301/308 to the nearest canonical slug URL with no chain and all internal links point to canonical URLs.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Unknown routes and unknown products return HTTP 200 soft-404 shell responses.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Unknown URLs return real 404/410 with safe noindex content and no misleading canonical or Product schema.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: E-3 accessibility and SXO findings remain open for unnamed header button, checkout checkbox labels, catalog input labels, generic product buttons, product gallery controls, and mobile evidence.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Rendered accessibility and customer journey smoke passes on desktop and mobile with stable labels, focus, states, and no ambiguous high-impact actions.

blockersCarriedForward.blocker: Cloudflare, Search Console, GBP, Merchant Center, GA4/analytics, server logs, field Core Web Vitals, and owner evidence are unavailable.
blockersCarriedForward.verification: Authenticated least-privilege readbacks confirm properties/accounts, submitted assets, diagnostics, alerts, field data, and evidence ownership.

eachRelease: Run local gates: static-assets:check, sitemap:check, feed:check, route-migration:check, private-routes:check, checkout:check, commerce:check.

Run bounded live HTTP matrix for homepage, service hub, service detail, contact, location, catalog, product slug, product legacy identifiers, legacy service/catalog aliases, checkout, private routes, unknown routes, robots, sitemap, product sitemap, and Merchant feed.
Run rendered browser smoke for homepage, service booking, contact, catalog, product, cart, checkout, private route, and unknown route.
Annotate release date/time, branch/commit, affected templates, Cloudflare/Figma Make deployment, static assets, redirect changes, schema/feed changes, provider deployments, and exceptions.
weekly: Check critical production asset URLs and redirect samples.
Review Search Console manual actions, security issues, indexing, sitemap, page experience, and enhancement reports when access exists.
Review Merchant Center diagnostics, Business Profile edits/suspension/hours, booking/order/checkout failures, and private-route alerts.
monthly: Segment Search Console and analytics by page type, locale, device, country, query intent, product/service family, inventory state, and qualified outcome.
Review product/feed stock and price drift, local facts, content freshness, service claim evidence, accessibility regression queue, field Core Web Vitals, and conversion quality.
Refresh representative URL set when a new page type, language route, product state, service state, or provider integration ships.
quarterly: Recheck structured data feature status, Merchant Center policy, GBP policy, crawler/AI policy, schema support, and documentation freshness.
Review route architecture, stale redirects, content portfolio, owner evidence, experiment decisions, privacy/accessibility/legal boundaries, and source freshness.

owners.area: Production host, Cloudflare Pages/Functions, redirects, status policy, static assets
alertTriggers: legacy redirect returns 200 or wrong target
public canonical route raw metadata missing
owners.verification: Public HTTP matrix and provider readback pass.

owners.area: Public/private route boundary, account/CMS/admin access, security/privacy
owners.verification: Unauthenticated route probes and security review pass.

owners.area: Search Console, indexing, sitemap submission, URL inspection, traffic anomaly diagnosis
owners.verification: Search Console readback and release annotation are recorded.

owners.area: Merchant feed, product facts, price, stock, schema/feed/cart/checkout parity
owners.verification: Feed diagnostics, product samples, and checkout reconciliation pass.
Analytics and business-system reconciliation pass with limitations documented.

owners.area: Local facts, GBP, citations, service evidence, reviews, media, legal and policy claims
Accessibility, SXO, mobile customer journeys, browser-agent operability
owners.verification: Browser/accessibility matrix passes on desktop and mobile.

incidentRunbook.trigger: Private/admin/account route exposes public 200 shell or private data
first15Minutes: Freeze deployments and preserve sanitized evidence.
Confirm unauthenticated reproduction in private/incognito context.
Disable or protect route at edge if private content is visible.
firstHour: Read recent deployment and route-policy changes.
Patch Cloudflare/status/auth policy or rollback to last safe build.
If data exposure is confirmed, escalate to privacy/legal and follow incident obligations.
incidentRunbook.verification: Unauthenticated routes return 401/403/safe 404, no private content, noindex where applicable.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: Any confirmed private data exposure or unauthenticated admin surface.

incidentRunbook.trigger: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, product sitemap, or Merchant feed missing, HTML, blocked, or wrong content type
first15Minutes: Confirm live HTTP status/content type from the canonical host.
Compare repo src/public assets and build output.
Check deployment target, static asset routing, cache, and edge fallback order.
firstHour: Deploy the correct static asset/routing fix or rollback to last known good deployment.
Purge relevant CDN cache if safe.
Submit/retry sitemap/feed fetch in platform tools when access exists.
incidentRunbook.verification: Assets return expected status, content type, and body; local and live checks agree.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: Canonical production cannot serve critical static SEO assets within the incident window.

incidentRunbook.trigger: Material drop in organic clicks/impressions/indexed pages or template-level visibility
first15Minutes: Check analytics/Search Console data freshness, timezone, filters, consent, and tracking changes.
Scope decline by page type, query intent, country, device, language, and search appearance.
Correlate with release annotations and production drift baseline.
firstHour: Check robots, noindex, canonical, redirects, raw/rendered HTML, sitemaps, manual actions, security issues, and platform status.
Separate demand, SERP, competitor, content, inventory, and measurement explanations before broad edits.
Patch only confirmed root cause or document hypothesis and next checkpoint.
incidentRunbook.verification: Segmented report shows affected scope, ruled-out causes, confirmed cause or open hypotheses, and next measurement checkpoint.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: A release-introduced blocker/critical technical regression aligns with the decline.

incidentRunbook.trigger: Merchant Center disapproval spike, feed fetch failure, or price/availability mismatch
first15Minutes: Check feed URL status/content type and latest generated feed timestamp.
Sample affected item IDs across website page, Product schema, feed, cart, and checkout.
Identify whether mismatch is product source, feed generation, deploy, cache, or checkout pricing.
firstHour: Regenerate/redeploy feed or fix source contract.
Pause affected promotion/scaling if product terms are unreliable.
Record Merchant Center diagnostics when access exists.
incidentRunbook.verification: Sampled items reconcile across feed, page, schema, cart, checkout, and provider diagnostics.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: False price, false availability, unsupported fitment, or checkout-term mismatch is live.

incidentRunbook.trigger: Booking, cart, checkout, or Paytrail callback journey fails or records misleading revenue/conversion data
first15Minutes: Confirm whether the issue is UI, provider, edge function, analytics event, or business-system reconciliation.
Avoid test payments or destructive submissions unless explicitly approved.
Check noindex/canonical status and customer-facing recovery messaging.
firstHour: Fix or rollback the failing journey, provider function, or event mapping.
Reconcile server-confirmed Paytrail state before reporting purchase outcomes.
incidentRunbook.verification: Checkout/browser smoke and server/provider readback pass; events use booking_submitted and server-confirmed purchase semantics.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: Customers cannot complete purchase/booking or revenue events become materially misleading.

incidentRunbook.trigger: Accessibility or mobile journey regression blocks booking, contact, product selection, cart, or checkout
first15Minutes: Reproduce on affected route/device with keyboard and browser accessibility tree.
Identify whether a shared component, modal, form control, or route chunk changed.
Confirm no high-impact action is ambiguous or irreversible.
firstHour: Patch accessible name/label/focus/state or rollback shared component change.
Run desktop and mobile browser smoke.
Record any temporary exception with owner, mitigation, and expiry.
incidentRunbook.verification: All visible controls have stable accessible names, fields have labels/errors/autocomplete where applicable, and primary journey completes safely.
incidentRunbook.rollbackCriteria: Booking, contact, product/cart, or checkout cannot be operated by keyboard or clear accessible controls.

rollbackAndCommunication.freezeTrigger: Any P0/BLOCKER or confirmed release-introduced P1/CRITICAL regression affecting crawl access, private route protection, product/checkout truth, or customer task completion.
defaultRollbackPath: Preserve sanitized evidence and exact timestamps.
Identify last known good deployment/source state.
Rollback or patch the smallest edge/runtime/source change required.
Re-run local gates, live HTTP matrix, and affected browser/platform checks.
Annotate the incident and update the baseline, tests, and owner register.
communicationPath: Incident owner opens the decision log with timestamp, severity, affected scope, and evidence.
Engineering owner handles technical mitigation and rollback.
SEO/growth ops owner records Search Console/platform implications and release annotation.
Business/local/commerce owner approves any public fact, price, policy, or claim exception.
Privacy/security/legal reviewer is pulled in for private data, consent, payment, legal, or unsupported-claim risk.

nextReviewTriggers: Production host changes from Figma-hosted runtime to Cloudflare Pages/repo build output.
Cloudflare, Search Console, GBP, Merchant Center, GA4, server logs, or field CWV access becomes available.
Static SEO assets, edge redirects, private route policy, or raw metadata injection changes.
Product feed, product schema, checkout, Paytrail, booking, service content, local facts, or legal/policy copy changes.
Before paid acquisition, guide/content scaling, SEO experiments, or local/product growth campaigns begin.

verification.command: node --input-type=module <<'NODE' ... E-4 live HTTP bounded baseline ... NODE

next: E-5 - Final Growth Readiness Classification And Board Wrap-Up
noGuarantee: This monitoring system improves implementation quality, regression detection, and incident discipline. It does not guarantee crawling, indexing, selected canonicals, rankings, rich results, local-pack visibility, Merchant Center approval, traffic, conversions, revenue, ROI, or AI inclusion.
